# Domain Knowledge Scope - High-Altitude Work Platform

## Overview
This platform serves as a professional networking and job marketplace specifically designed for the high-altitude work industry, including rope access technicians, industrial climbers, wind turbine technicians, and other height work specialists.

## Core Domain Concepts

### 1. User Types
- **Contractors/Freelancers**: Individual professionals offering high-altitude services
- **Companies**: Organizations seeking high-altitude work specialists
- **Training Providers**: Organizations offering certifications and training courses
- **Equipment Suppliers**: Companies providing specialized equipment and gear

### 2. Professional Roles & Specializations

#### Rope Access Technicians
- **IRATA Levels**: Level 1 (Trainee), Level 2 (Technician), Level 3 (Team Leader/Supervisor)
- **SPRAT Levels**: Level 1, Level 2, Level 3 (equivalent to IRATA)
- **Specializations**: 
  - Industrial cleaning
  - Inspection and testing
  - Maintenance and repair
  - Installation work
  - Non-destructive testing (NDT)

#### Wind Energy Technicians
- **GWO Certifications**: Basic Safety Training (BST), Basic Technical Training (BTT)
- **Blade Repair Specialists**: Composite repair, leading edge protection
- **Electrical Technicians**: High voltage work, control systems

#### Industrial Climbers
- **Building Maintenance**: Window cleaning, facade inspection
- **Infrastructure**: Bridge inspection, tower maintenance
- **Construction**: High-rise construction support

#### Specialized Roles
- **Confined Space Entry**: Tank inspection, vessel maintenance
- **Underwater Welding**: Offshore platforms, marine structures
- **Arborists**: Tree surgery, urban forestry
- **Emergency Response**: Rescue operations, disaster response

### 3. Certifications & Training

#### International Standards
- **IRATA** (Industrial Rope Access Trade Association)
- **SPRAT** (Society of Professional Rope Access Technicians)
- **GWO** (Global Wind Organisation)
- **OSHA** compliance certifications

#### Safety Certifications
- **First Aid/CPR**: Workplace emergency response
- **Rescue Training**: Self-rescue and team rescue
- **Fall Protection**: Harness use, anchor points
- **Working at Height**: Risk assessment, safety protocols

#### Technical Certifications
- **NDT Methods**: Visual testing, magnetic particle, ultrasonic
- **Welding Certifications**: Various processes and positions
- **Electrical Safety**: High voltage, LOTO procedures
- **Confined Space**: Entry procedures, atmospheric monitoring

### 4. Equipment & Tools

#### Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
- **Harnesses**: Full body, sit harnesses, rescue harnesses
- **Helmets**: Industrial climbing helmets with chin straps
- **Gloves**: Cut-resistant, grip-enhanced work gloves
- **Safety Footwear**: Non-slip, steel-toed boots

#### Rope Access Equipment
- **Ropes**: Static lines, dynamic ropes, rescue ropes
- **Hardware**: Carabiners, descenders, ascenders, pulleys
- **Anchoring**: Anchor points, rigging plates, slings
- **Fall Protection**: Energy absorbers, positioning lanyards

#### Specialized Tools
- **Inspection Equipment**: Borescopes, ultrasonic thickness gauges
- **Cleaning Tools**: Pressure washers, specialized brushes
- **Repair Tools**: Composite repair kits, welding equipment
- **Access Equipment**: Rope access kits, suspended platforms

### 5. Work Environments & Industries

#### Industrial Sectors
- **Oil & Gas**: Refineries, offshore platforms, storage tanks
- **Power Generation**: Wind turbines, power plants, transmission lines
- **Maritime**: Ships, ports, offshore structures
- **Manufacturing**: Factories, warehouses, industrial facilities

#### Infrastructure
- **Buildings**: High-rise maintenance, facade work
- **Bridges**: Inspection, maintenance, repair
- **Towers**: Communication towers, water towers
- **Monuments**: Historic preservation, cleaning

#### Environmental Conditions
- **Weather Considerations**: Wind limits, temperature ranges
- **Height Classifications**: Low level (2-6m), medium level (6-30m), high level (30m+)
- **Access Challenges**: Confined spaces, over water, remote locations

### 6. Safety & Risk Management

#### Risk Assessment
- **Job Hazard Analysis (JHA)**: Pre-work risk identification
- **Method Statements**: Detailed work procedures
- **Rescue Plans**: Emergency response procedures
- **Environmental Factors**: Weather, terrain, access routes

#### Safety Standards
- **Fall Protection Standards**: ANSI Z359, EN 363
- **Rope Access Standards**: IRATA ICOP, SPRAT Safe Practices
- **Industry-Specific**: OSHA 1926 Subpart M, API standards

### 7. Project Types & Service Categories

#### Maintenance Services
- **Preventive Maintenance**: Scheduled inspections, routine servicing
- **Corrective Maintenance**: Repair work, component replacement
- **Emergency Repairs**: Urgent response, safety-critical issues

#### Inspection Services
- **Visual Inspections**: General condition assessment
- **NDT Services**: Detailed structural analysis
- **Compliance Inspections**: Regulatory requirement fulfillment

#### Installation & Construction
- **Equipment Installation**: Mounting, positioning, securing
- **Structural Work**: Welding, bolting, assembly
- **System Integration**: Electrical, mechanical connections

### 8. Business & Legal Considerations

#### Licensing & Insurance
- **Professional Licenses**: State/regional requirements
- **Insurance Coverage**: Liability, workers compensation
- **Bonding Requirements**: Performance bonds, payment bonds
- **Safety Certifications**: Company safety ratings

#### Contractual Aspects
- **Service Agreements**: Scope of work, deliverables
- **Safety Requirements**: Client-specific safety protocols
- **Quality Standards**: Workmanship guarantees, warranties
- **Emergency Response**: Call-out procedures, response times

### 9. Geographic & Seasonal Factors

#### Regional Variations
- **Climate Considerations**: Seasonal work limitations
- **Regional Standards**: Local safety regulations
- **Market Demands**: Industry concentration areas
- **Travel Requirements**: Mobilization, accommodation needs

#### Availability Patterns
- **Seasonal Work**: Weather-dependent scheduling
- **Project Duration**: Short-term vs. long-term assignments
- **Emergency Availability**: 24/7 response capabilities
- **Planned Outages**: Scheduled maintenance windows

## Platform-Specific Features

### User Profile Elements
- **Professional Experience**: Years in industry, project history
- **Certifications**: Current and expiring certifications
- **Availability Calendar**: Date ranges, geographic preferences
- **Equipment Inventory**: Owned tools and equipment
- **Safety Record**: Incident-free hours, safety awards

### Search & Matching Criteria
- **Skill-Based Matching**: Specific technical abilities
- **Certification Requirements**: Mandatory vs. preferred qualifications
- **Geographic Radius**: Travel distance limitations
- **Availability Overlap**: Schedule compatibility
- **Experience Level**: Minimum years, project complexity

### Job Posting Categories
- **Urgency Level**: Emergency, urgent, planned
- **Duration**: Single day, short-term, long-term contracts
- **Location Type**: Urban, remote, offshore
- **Safety Requirements**: Client-specific protocols
- **Equipment Provision**: Client-provided vs. contractor-supplied

This domain knowledge scope provides the foundation for building a comprehensive platform that addresses the unique needs of the high-altitude work industry while maintaining focus on safety, professionalism, and specialized expertise.
